question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the `deleted is false` predicate in the native query is evaluated after the range scan (not as a leading index condition), causing the database to read and discard a large number of deleted rows and wasting I/O resources, resulting in a non-network-based DoS when many deleted rows exist?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a non-network-based DoS by exploiting the fact that the Pageable sort field (registered_node_id) is passed as a string to the native query, and if the sort column is not indexed, causing the database to perform a full table scan followed by an in-memory sort for every request?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the native query returns a List<RegisteredNode> (an eager collection) rather than a lazy stream, causing the entire result set to be deserialized into Java objects before any response is sent, and by requesting large ranges, cause heap exhaustion and a non-network-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `@> array[:type]::smallint[]` operator by supplying a type value that is not present in any row, causing PostgreSQL to scan the entire table to confirm no matches exist, wasting CPU and I/O resources and causing a non-network-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged user cause a non-network-based DoS by exploiting the fact that the Pageable object's page size (limit) is the only bound on result set size, and if MAX_LIMIT is set to a large value, requesting MAX_LIMIT rows from a full-range query causes the application to allocate a proportionally large List<RegisteredNode> on every request?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the native query uses `select *` (all columns) rather than projecting only needed fields, causing the JDBC driver to transfer and deserialize all column data for every matching row, wasting network bandwidth between the application and database and causing a non-network-based DoS through excessive data transfer?
